hero.question: Avalanche vs. Penguins
hero.prob_take: At 59.5%, the market is barely leaning Avalanche despite them being the stronger team on paper, which signals sharp money hasn't fully committed to either side yet. The +2.0 point move on modest volume ($468K) looks like retail following the narrative rather than informed positioning, making this a trap for anyone chasing the recent momentum.

daily_take.headline: Avalanche money is flowing in. Here's why the 59% ask is still a steal.
daily_take.deck: Avalanche just cracked 59.5% on Polymarket, up 2 points in 24 hours on modest $468K volume. That's smart money recognizing what Vegas already priced in: Colorado's elite goaltending and top-5 power play matchup badly against Pittsburgh's third-worst penalty kill. The real move is waiting for the line to tick higher into the 62-65 range before the puck drops, then selling the spike. Sharp bettors should be fading the recency bias here, not chasing it.

sidebar.headline: Iran war odds dropped half a point but $18.3M volume says institutional capital still sees black swan risk. Watch for any Israel escalation noise to reverse this quickly.

sidebar.headline: Arizona's tournament odds jumped 2.2 points on a $865K volume spike. Shallow market, overweight recency of one good week. Long-term value play if they cool off.
sidebar.label: Arizona 2026 Tournament: 20.3%

sidebar.headline: Anduril IPO crashed 22 points in one day. Smells like one whale trade or private intel on funding delays. Too much volatility, too little volume to chase right now.
